content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory architectures can be repurposed to implement a tiered, self-pruning "curiosity buffer" that only retains high-surprise, high-leverage unexpected observations for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning agents, solving the longstanding problem of noisy, redundant curiosity signals that degrade long-horizon task performance. This design leverages the core active learning property of prioritizing only the most informative unlabeled samples to actively curate which new exploration experiences get stored long-term for the agent to learn from.
